programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the concluding part of his trip to the Mill Street Bistro in Norwalk, Ohio, Gordon's disagreement with proprietor Joe Nagy erupts into one of his most intense confrontations yet. Just when it looks like he has turned the corner and is ready to accept help, Joe slips back into his old ways, denying his own failings and picking petty fights. Can Gordon get this stubborn owner to finally accept his advice and secure the future of his restaurant?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Yanni's in Seattle, where he clashes with defiant owner Peter Avgoustiou, a chef so set in his ways that he hasn't allowed any changes to his menu since the Greek restaurant opened in 1984. Peter's family is concerned the business is becoming trapped in the past, and standards are slipping, but he seems determined to stick to his guns

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: A lie spirals into a gigantic conflict for one couple, and with revelations happening left, right and centre, all of the relationships start shifting in new directions

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitch and Cam worry about the growing cost of their wedding, so they agree to each sell a prized possession to earn some extra cash. Jay, Gloria and Manny challenge one another to step outside their comfort zones, involving traditional sausages, pickle and Stella's belly, while the Dunphy kids are in a mischievous mood after finding an old answering machine recording of Claire breaking the news that she's pregnant

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mitch and Cam prepare to walk down the aisle, but are faced with a host of hitches before they tie the knot. Pepper is forced to bring the ceremony forward by four hours because of a spreading wildfire, while one of the grooms' tuxedos has been left at the dry cleaners

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Glamorous Hollywood star Sara Sloane visits Springfield and becomes infatuated with Ned Flanders, who finds himself asking Homer for dating advice. Featuring the voices of Helen Fielding and Marisa Tomei

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: With changes afoot at Springfield's airport, Bart persuades Krusty to run for Congress so he can redirect the flight path of planes passing over the Simpson home. However, he soon alters his policies once elected

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer manages to become the owner of the power plant and, in a rare display of intelligence and ingenuity, he celebrates his new-found prestige by sacking former boss Mr Burns - who does not take kindly to being ousted

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: 'Scuse Me While I Miss the Sky
programme.desc: Lisa resolves to pursue a career in astronomy, but unfortunately her decision coincides with Chief Wiggum's hare-brained new initiative to reduce crime in Springfield - by illuminating the whole town all night. With the guest voice of Eric Idle

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta tries to set a new record on a training course, Diaz does not receive an invitation to Boyle's wedding, and Holt becomes addicted to a game application on his smartphone. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Boyle asks Peralta to be his best man, but as well as helping out with cake-tasting and choosing invitations, he must also persuade the groom to start standing up to Vivian before the big day. Holt helps Diaz make a sincere apology and Jeffords, Santiago and Gina embark on a tough new diet - but struggle to stick to it

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay switches sides after joining Cam's bowling team, while Phil and Claire see a different side to their neighbours. American comedy following the lives of three dysfunctional households - a man who struggles to keep up with his younger wife, an embarrassing husband and a gay couple getting used to parenthood after adopting a Vietnamese baby. Starring Ed O'Neill and Julie Bowen

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Bad moods must be catching when Phil and Jay spend the afternoon together. Meanwhile, Gloria teaches Haley how to stand up to her boss. Starring Ed O'Neill and Julie Bowen

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: While Alex goes head-to-head with a school rival, Gloria prepares for her US citizenship test, only to be thrown off her game by Javier. American comedy following the lives of three dysfunctional households - a man who struggles to keep up with his younger wife, an embarrassing husband and a gay couple getting used to parenthood after adopting a Vietnamese baby. Starring Ed O'Neill and Julie Bowen

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire is in a quandary after receiving a job offer from a lucrative hotel chain, but her decision is made easier by Jay's attitude to her potential departure. Manny is recovering after a trip to the dentist, prompting Gloria to take advantage of his groggy state to try to engineer a break-up between him and his girlfriend

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Ned Flanders buys the Simpsons' home when they are unable to pay their mortgage and rents it back to them at an affordable rate, but Homer soon takes advantage of his new landlord's generosity

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa has to go undercover as a nun and infiltrate a convent when Maggie goes missing after being left on its doorstep. Meanwhile, Marge is left temporarily blind when she stares at a total eclipse, so Homer has to sort out their rat-infested kitchen

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: In the Name of the Grandfather
programme.desc: After ducking out of a bonding session with Grampa, a guilt-ridden Homer vows to make it up to his father by granting him a wish. The old man decides to visit Ireland and drop in on his favourite pub from his army days. Upon arrival, the pair discover the bar is a shadow of its former self, and agree to buy the business and turn its fortunes around

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer proposes to Marge for the second time when he discovers their marriage licence is invalid, but the ceremony does not proceed without a hitch. With the voices of Dan Castellaneta and Julie Kavner

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Milhouse moves to Capital City with his mother, forcing Bart to look for new friends - but he finds himself bonding more strongly with Lisa. Meanwhile, Homer resorts to begging to raise money for Marge's anniversary gift

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa schemes to get her own back on Bart for destroying the prize tomato she grew for a science project. Meanwhile, Homer is arrested for drink-driving and finds himself having to attend Alcoholics Anonymous meetings

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: With a fractious atmosphere in the air, the couples head off for a weekend retreat. And there are big questions looming for all of the newlyweds

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: It's the end-of-agency party, and a surprise from the agents tests the strength of the connections that the celebrities and their dates have formed. Professor Green takes the mic for a performance to remember

programme.title: Bad Teacher (2011)
programme.desc: Cynical, hard-drinking teacher Elizabeth Halsey hates her job and the kids in her class, and hopes to escape her daily grind by landing a rich husband. A wealthy substitute seems like a suitable candidate, but she has a rival for his attention in a squeaky-clean colleague. Comedy, starring Cameron Diaz, Justin Timberlake, Jason Segel and Lucy Punch

programme.title: Friends with Benefits (2011)
programme.desc: An art director joins the staff of a magazine, and is soon sleeping with the recruiter who hired him. Both are determined to avoid a relationship and resolve to keep their feelings out of the arrangement - which proves harder than expected. Romantic comedy, starring Justin Timberlake, Mila Kunis, Patricia Clarkson and Woody Harrelson

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Fitness fanatic Jade, 24, sits down for a meal with Jak, 29, a yoga lover in touch with his spiritual side and who shares her love of exercise and tattoos, while fashion stylist Christine, 70, and entrepreneur Peter, 71, share stories of the Swinging Sixties. Health club manager Donna, 51, is a born romantic with old fashioned values at heart and her date is 53-year-old stetson-wearing Simon from Dallas, Texas, who's happy to stand out from the crowd and is looking for a strong woman to stand by his side

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: It's the end-of-agency party, and a surprise from the agents tests the strength of the connections that the celebrities and their dates have formed. Professor Green takes the mic for a performance to remember

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: With a fractious atmosphere in the air, the couples head off for a weekend retreat. And there are big questions looming for all of the newlyweds

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly complains that Pops is spending too much time with her at home, so Murray takes him to work at the furniture shop. Meanwhile, Adam reluctantly gives away his old toys in an effort to make him look more grown up, hoping it will result in a kiss with Dana. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly is pitted against a rival mother when she tries to micro-manage Barry and Erica's search for college places, while Erica tells Pops that she has tried to enhance her prospects by lying about her extracurricular activities. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride: Revisited
programme.desc: Bride Lauren and music-mad Perry are under the spotlight as they regroup with best man Michael and bridesmaid Leigh to relive their gig-themed wedding at The Forum, Kentish Town

programme.title: Lego Masters Australia
programme.desc: The LEGO Masters teams help Hamish pitch brand-new sports to be included in the Olympic Games by building visual representations of the wacky new Olympic sports

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: Featuring a variety of cats doing strange things, soldiers stuck in the sand and a dog balancing on a rope

programme.title: The Amazing Race Australia
programme.desc: Deep in Borneo, the four remaining teams battle to reach the grand finale. After breakfast with the orangutans, they raft rapids, dodge crocodiles, test their blowpipe skills and race to the finish

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: With the couples' Panama honeymoons drawing to a close, it's time for them to face up to the reality of everyday life with their new spouses. Some of them are excited to start planning for the future, while others are already craving more personal space. And one bride's attempt at intimacy takes an unexpected - and extremely awkward - turn

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: The newlyweds return from their honeymoon in Panama and are immediately brought back to earth with a bump when they move in together and start facing the challenges of real life. Also, a heated argument could prove disastrous for one couple's budding wedded bliss

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: After an explosive meeting with an expert, one couple are left to pick up the pieces of their already fragile relationship, ut there is still a grain of hope for one loved-up pair

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. To make matters worse, the restaurant's food is less than appetising and its long-serving chef seems to be incompetent

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Baltimore, Maryland, where Denise Whiting has alienated customers by trademarking the word 'hon', a term of endearment used by the local community. When she opened Café Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of Denise's unwavering business ambition, people started to boycott the restaurant, leaving her frustrated by falling sales

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer has a heart attack but cannot afford proper medical care, so dodgy doctor Nick Riviera offers to perform a cut-price triple-bypass operation. Cartoon capers, with the voices of Dan Castellaneta and Hank Azaria

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge sets out to halt the plans of an unscrupulous businessman intending to defraud Springfield's residents by supplying them with a defective monorail system. Featuring the guest voice of Leonard Nimoy

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Selma decides to take her Aunt Gladys's advice and start a family - but a day in charge of Bart and Lisa at a beer-themed amusement park soon changes her mind

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart grows frustrated by Homer's lack of interest in him and sets out to search for a new father figure. Lisa tries to break her addiction to an expensive teenage chat-line, but finds it harder than expected

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa schemes to get her own back on Bart for destroying the prize tomato she grew for a science project. Meanwhile, Homer is arrested for drink-driving and finds himself having to attend Alcoholics Anonymous meetings

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: At the Springfield library, Marge stimulates the children's interest in history by relating extraordinary tales featuring the wives of Henry VIII, the Lewis and Clark expedition and the early life of Austrian composer Mozart

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Milhouse moves to Capital City with his mother, forcing Bart to look for new friends - but he finds himself bonding more strongly with Lisa. Meanwhile, Homer resorts to begging to raise money for Marge's anniversary gift

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge stages a revival of a musical from high school, but her pleasant memories are threatened by the return of an old rival

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Grampa falls victim to a phone scam, the rest of the Simpsons set out to get his money back. With the guest voice of Alan Cumming

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Another selection of spooky stories, including parodies of Bambi and Parasite, and a tale of a tree coming to life after Homer chops it down

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Moe is faced with a difficult choice when Evergreen Terrace develops a traffic problem

programme.title: Dog (2022)
programme.desc: US Army Ranger Briggs is determined to regain his place at Joint Base Lewis-McChord in Washington. His superior officer agrees to return him to the fold if he can successfully escort a military working dog named Lulu along America's Pacific Coast to attend the funeral of her handler. Comedy, co-directed by and starring Channing Tatum

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A compilation of the best moments of series seven, featuring a rolling cast of famous faces as they tune into the best TV on the box. The series saw the likes of Vicky Pattison and Pete Wicks, Clare Balding and Alice Arnold, Denise van Outen and Johnny Vaughan, and Jordan Banjo and Perri Kiely cast their eye on the televisual delights on offer

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on the Eurovision Song Contest, Faking It, Virgin Island, The Four Seasons, Night Coppers, Inside Our Minds, Naked and Afraid, and Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions and the lively discussions that are prompted by the week's biggest and best shows

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including Strictly Come Dancing, Our Dementia Choir Sings Again with Vicky McClure, First Dates Hotel, My Possessed Pet, This Morning, The Ex-Wife and Sue Perkins: Perfectly Legal. Plus, thoughts on ITV News' of possible power shortages. First shown in October 2022

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Dina, 39, is keen to settle down and start a family, but her date, 41-year-old Stephen, is a born adventurer who has climbed the Matterhorn and swum oceans. Will he run a mile when she shares her intentions with him? Margaret, 73, hasn't been on a date for 44 years and is looking for a family man who shares her love of music and life, while art student Rachel, meets vegan singer-songwriter and self-confessed hippie Hari

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on the Eurovision Song Contest, Faking It, Virgin Island, The Four Seasons, Night Coppers, Inside Our Minds, Naked and Afraid, and Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions and the lively discussions that are prompted by the week's biggest and best shows

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three previous restaurants he tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he heads for West Babylon, New York, where he helped the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts, before returning to Davide in Boston, an Italian restaurant run by two warring brothers. Finally, he visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island, run by hot-headed owner Abby

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: David questions whether Anthony has doubts, Gabby works out if she had a future with Joel, PK attempts to show a different side to his character

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: A lie spirals into a gigantic conflict for one couple, and with revelations happening left, right and centre, all of the relationships start shifting in new directions

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay's next stop is Philadelphia, where family run restaurant Chiarella's is struggling to survive in an area full of thriving Italian eateries. Tommy and Dina Defino's marriage is on the verge of collapse and his sense of personal failure only adds to the troubles of their crisis-ridden business

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica's new job at the department store is put in jeopardy when Beverly takes advantage of her daughter's employee discount too frequently. Meanwhile, during a trip to the horse racing track, Pops bends the truth to convince Barry that his bad luck has changed for the better. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly and Murray fall out over Barry's plan to perform his martial arts routine at the annual high school talent show. Pops buys Erica an electric guitar, urging her to take to the stage and sing. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride: Revisited
programme.desc: Steph and Billy, their best man George and bridesmaid Becca relive the unforgettable piggy wedding that shocked many back in 2016

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire finally gets Phil to agree to sell their old car, Mitchell decides to build a large princess castle for Lily, and Manny invites a girl over for a study date - which makes Gloria uncomfortable. American comedy following the lives of three dysfunctional households - a man who struggles to keep up with his younger wife, an embarrassing husband and a gay couple getting used to parenthood after adopting a Vietnamese baby. Starring Julie Bowen and Ty Burrell

programme.title: Modern Family

Comedy, starring Ed O'Neill

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube

When she opened Café Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of Denise's unwavering business ambition, people started to boycott the restaurant, leaving her frustrated by falling sales

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer has a heart attack but cannot afford proper medical care, so dodgy doctor Nick Riviera offers to perform a cut-price triple-bypass operation. Cartoon capers, with the voices of Dan Castellaneta and Hank Azaria

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge sets out to halt the plans of an unscrupulous businessman intending to defraud Springfield's residents by supplying them with a defective monorail system. Featuring the guest voice of Leonard Nimoy

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Selma decides to take her Aunt Gladys's advice and start a family - but a day in charge of Bart and Lisa at a beer-themed amusement park soon changes her mind

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart grows frustrated by Homer's lack of interest in him and sets out to search for a new father figure. Lisa tries to break her addiction to an expensive teenage chat-line, but finds it harder than expected

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa schemes to get her own back on Bart for destroying the prize tomato she grew for a science project. Meanwhile, Homer is arrested for drink-driving and finds himself having to attend Alcoholics Anonymous meetings

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: At the Springfield library, Marge stimulates the children's interest in history by relating extraordinary tales featuring the wives of Henry VIII, the Lewis and Clark expedition and the early life of Austrian composer Mozart

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Milhouse moves to Capital City with his mother, forcing Bart to look for new friends - but he finds himself bonding more strongly with Lisa. Meanwhile, Homer resorts to begging to raise money for Marge's anniversary gift

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Another selection of spooky stories, including parodies of Bambi and Parasite, and a tale of a tree coming to life after Homer chops it down

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Dog (2022)
programme.desc: US Army Ranger Briggs is determined to regain his place at Joint Base Lewis-McChord in Washington. His superior officer agrees to return him to the fold if he can successfully escort a military working dog named Lulu along America's Pacific Coast to attend the funeral of her handler. Comedy, co-directed by and starring Channing Tatum

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A compilation of the best moments of series seven, featuring a rolling cast of famous faces as they tune into the best TV on the box. programme.title: First Dates

programme.title: American Detective with Lt Joe Kenda
programme.desc: When a father of five is found dead on his kitchen floor, Det. James Colley must untangle a web of twisted relationships and bizarre fantasies to uncover the mystery

programme.title: Truth About Murder with Sunny Hostin
programme.desc: Former prosecutor Sunny Hostin re-examines a vintage crime in which two teenagers were brutally attacked by a stranger in a park in Portland, Texas. Helped by the survivor, police desperately hunted for the callous killer

programme.title: Kiss of Death
programme.desc: Seasoned EMT, Mario, meets Gina at the hospital they work at. Sparks fly and they marry, living a life of luxury. But in time the façade unravels, ending in murder

programme.title: Seduced to Slay
programme.desc: When a man's childhood friend moves in with him and his family, a sinister seduction leads to a cold-blooded murder in Buford, Georgia

programme.title: Fatal Affairs
programme.desc: When a woman meets a marine, they fall in love and start a life together. This perfect façade hides a dirty double life of drugs and deceit

programme.title: A Body in the Basement
programme.desc: A routine traffic stop leads to a deadly discovery in a suburban home in Massachusetts, and officers realise they've apprehended a serial killer

programme.title: Deadly Sins
programme.desc: How God-fearing Paul and Letha Westfall's involvement in daughter Kristen's bitter custody dispute turned a church into a bloody battlefield. Plus, how surgeon Dr John Christ's unusual fixation pushed his wife to the brink of divorce - but not before blood was shed

programme.title: Mean Girl Murders
programme.desc: A group of girls begin lifelong friendships at church camp. Life takes them down different roads and their bonds remain until one of them disappears

programme.title: Mean Girl Murders
programme.desc: When a Homecoming Queen and a Dairy Princess are murdered in Marathon County, Wisconsin, detectives question a pool of suspects, from strangers to frenemies

programme.title: Bride Killa
programme.desc: The death of newlywed wife Courtney Burton, who was killed by husband Robert Arvizu. The murderer also broke the nose of the man he accused of flirting with her

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Sci-fi disaster movie, starring Christopher Lloyd and Joel Gretsch

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: The Gorgon (1964)
programme.desc: A spate of strange deaths occurs in a small German town, with the bodies being turned to stone, and the proprietor of a nearby lunatic asylum soon finds himself under suspicion. However, a folklore expert realises a local woman has been possessed by the legendary snake-headed monster of Greek mythology, and has developed the ability to petrify her victims with a deadly gaze. Hammer horror, starring Peter Cushing, Christopher Lee, Barbara Shelley, Richard Pasco and Patrick Troughton

programme.title: Jules Verne's Rocket to the Moon (1967)
programme.desc: Legendary American showman Phineas T Barnum designs a space rocket, which he hopes will take him to the moon, but his scheme is threatened when two crooks get wind of his plan and decide to sabotage the craft. Sci-fi comedy, starring Terry-Thomas, Lionel Jeffries, Burl Ives, Gert Frobe, Dennis Price and Hermione Gingold

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: The unexpected presence of Klingon warriors on a mineral-rich world jeopardises delicate negotiations for a treaty with the Federation. Kirk and his crew end up trying to prevent a war and drive their deadliest foes from the planet. Guest starring Julie Newmar

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: The crime-fighting superhero is framed for murder by a killer he once helped put behind bars

programme.desc: The city is terrorised by a relentless serial killer known as the Strangler, who targets teenage runaways working as vice girls. Vintage American drama, starring William Shatner

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker is tasked with babysitting duties when Alex finds an abandoned baby. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: The Ice Road (2021)
programme.desc: After a remote diamond mine collapses in far northern Canada, a 'big-rig' ice road driver must lead an impossible rescue mission over a frozen lake to save the trapped miners. Action thriller starring Liam Neeson, Marcus Thomas and Laurence Fishburne.

programme.title: Daylight (1996)
programme.desc: A devastating explosion in the New Jersey Tunnel causes a horrific pile-up, trapping a group of motorists underground. A former emergency worker witnesses the blast and heads in to guide the handful of survivors to safety before the waters of the Hudson river above them break through and flood the tunnel. Disaster movie, starring Sylvester Stallone, Amy Brenneman, Viggo Mortensen, Dan Hedaya and Claire Bloom.

programme.title: Armed Response (2017)
programme.desc: A military unit attempting to find out what killed their predecessors find themselves trapped in a compound as strange events begin to take place. Action thriller, starring Wesley Snipes, Anne Heche and Dave Annable

programme.title: Paydirt (2020)
programme.desc: A man recently released from prison teams up with his old crew to find a buried bag of cash stolen a decade ago, while being tracked by a retired Sheriff. Crime thriller, starring Val Kilmer

programme.title: In Harm's Way (1965)
programme.desc: Two Navy officers are disgraced in the aftermath of the attack on Pearl Harbour, one for a disastrous engagement with the enemy and the other for a drunken brawl after learning of his wife's death. They get another chance to prove their worth when they are placed in charge of a vital counter-offensive. Second World War drama, starring John Wayne, Kirk Douglas and Henry Fonda

programme.title: Triple Cross (1966)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War spy adventure about Eddie Chapman, a bank robber who became a British double agent in exchange for money and a full pardon. Following the German invasion of Jersey, Chapman offers to provide information to the Nazis, who are unaware his loyalties really lie with the British Secret Service. With Christopher Plummer, Trevor Howard, Yul Brynner, Romy Schneider and Claudine Auger

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.desc: A man is transported back in time _ seconds after musing on the idea of predicting the future. Drama, starring Russell Johnson

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.desc: A strange old man causes much distress when he gives his haunted automobile to a car dealer. Jack Carson stars

programme.title: The Young Americans (1993)
programme.desc: Stylish thriller, starring Harvey Keitel as a hard-bitten New York cop, assigned to Scotland Yard to help combat Europe's increasing drug problem, who has his own secret agenda for tracking down crime kingpin Viggo Mortensen. With Iain Glen, Craig Kelly and Keith Allen

programme.title: The Way of the Gun (2000)
programme.desc: A woman is paid $1million to carry the child of a ruthless gangster, only to be abducted by two inexperienced kidnappers who haul her off to Mexico, little realising to what lengths the crook will go to ensure her safe return. Thriller, starring Benicio Del Toro, James Caan, Ryan Phillippe and Juliette Lewis

programme.title: Born to Raise Hell (2010)
programme.desc: An Interpol agent investigating a gang of Russian gun smugglers gets caught up in a shoot-out that leaves his partner dead. Swearing vengeance, he goes on the rampage through the Eastern European underworld to track down the gang members and carry out his own brand of justice. Action thriller, starring Steven Seagal and Dan Badarau

programme.title: Never Let Go (2015)
programme.desc: A woman on holiday in Morocco takes the law into her own hands after her baby is kidnapped, but ends up framed for murder. Thriller, with Angela Dixon and Nigel Whitmey

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Greg, 47, is airlifted to St George's with critical injuries to his arm and leg after being knocked off his motorbike on the way into work. As his wife Lucie rushes to his bedside, she's shocked by what she finds. Cameras also follow, Cara, 11, who has fallen from the top of a human pyramid doing gymnastics and has a broken arm that doctors must try to realign

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A motorcyclist collides with a deer. Luckily for the patient, an orthopaedic surgeon stops to help. A woman driving to work is forced to pull over and call 999 after experiencing heart palpitations. A young man is constantly being sick and needs to go to hospital. A bike ride ends with a bruised bottom the likes of which the paramedics have never seen before, and after seven weeks with an infection an asthma patient is still struggling to breathe

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Brooke is rushed to St George's after suffering an asthma attack so severe that the paramedic team had to put her in an induced coma at the scene. Doctors are concerned that she may have dangerous amounts of fluid on her lungs and decide to take urgent chest X-rays. Howard, who's 76, arrives with burns to his face, hands and airways after trying to put out an explosion in his garden shed. As his worried wife waits for news in the relatives' room, doctors attempt to stabilise his condition and investigate the extent of his injuries

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A look at the specialist team dedicated to caring for people when they suffer the potentially devastating consequences of a stroke, including 77-year-old Alex. Husband Paul sits nervously at her bedside as they wait to see if the drug she has been given will bring back her speech and movement. Meanwhile, doctors examine 66-year-old Steven, who has been admitted with severe stomach cramps

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Three groups of campers explore The Highlands of Scotland. Their first stop is Aberfeldy Caravan Park on the river Tay, where a ceilidh and some whitewater rafting are on the cards

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: On the Wirral, Simon O'Brien has a crazy plan to turn a chunky chain into a standard lamp, while Henry Cole restores a 1963 Ford Anglia

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team is near Preston, where Simon O'Brien restores a spotlight he believes may be from a German U-boat while Henry Cole gets creative with an aircraft jump seat

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Claire Hilton, who runs Robertsbrook Guest House in Swanage, Dorset, feels she has a good chance of winning the title of best B&B as another round of the competition gets under way. However, guests Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome have doubts over the cleanliness of their bedroom, and another couple are accustomed to more opulent surroundings

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome hope to impress with their two-star B&B Eden Lodge in Falmouth, Cornwall. However, the state of their property disappoints their fellow competitors. At dinner, James and Maria Church reveal the truth about their famous daughter Charlotte, and the following morning's breakfast reduces some of the guests to fits of giggles

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It is the turn of Charlotte Church's parents James and Maria to play host at Dexby Townhouse in Cardiff, and they pull out all the stops by laying on a day of white-water rafting and a magic performance courtesy of their nephew Elliot. But the following morning, some of the guests are kept waiting for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Newlyweds Pip Easby and Nicky Lesser-Easby, who run Muddifords Court Country House in Devon, open their doors for the final stay of the competition. The Churches find their four-poster bed too small, and not all the guests enjoy their trip to a local glass-blowing factory, but feedback at check-out time leaves the hosts feeling confident

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. An underpayment from a surprising source changes the dynamics of the group and throws the competition wide open, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former property developer Ian hosts the first dinner party in Gloucestershire, hoping his super-healthy menu will win him the £1,000 prize - although it is possible his guests may be more impressed with his 12-bedroom mansion in the country. Some naked antics, a pinata and stories of millions pounds won and lost make it a night to remember

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Then-Miss Cheltenham Sophie hosts the second dinner party in Gloucestershire, hoping her evening of elegant food and pageantry will win her the £1,000 prize. The 24-year-old reveals what she does for a living to her guests, and one attendee makes a surprising confession of her own

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Rugby-loving Carmel hosts the third dinner party in Gloucestershire, and she hopes to impress with a 'Six Nations' starter, a pancetta, king prawn and chorizo main, and a Key lime pie for dessert. An attempt to charm her guests backfires when she likens Ian to a psychotic killer, and the atmosphere takes a further knock after Chris gets on the wrong side of Sophie

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: School teacher Polly, 40, hosts the fourth night of the competition in Gloucestershire, and hopes to impress her guests with a fine-dining experience. She begins with a seafood starter before serving up a meaty main course, and isn't unnerved by the fact she hasn't had any practise cooking either dish. However, her guests show off round the table and threaten to take the attention away from the night, but Ian is highly impressed with Polly's choice of entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the turn of exuberant hair salon owner Chris to host the final dinner party in Gloucestershire with home-made treats on the menu, including a tiger prawn starter, venison for the main and a dessert of chocolate and walnut ice-cream. Chat around the table reveals some differing opinions after the guests make a discovery in the host's living room, and the group reflect on their time together before the winner of the cash prize is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The latest competition begins in Norfolk at the Old Ram Coaching Inn, one of five-star-trained Victoria MacDonald's three B&B sites. Guests Michele and Cheryl aren't impressed with Victoria's dusty towel rail and a suspicious brown smudge in the toilet. However, Aaron and Mel aren't happy about a hairy bed. At breakfast, Aaron fumes over his eggs, and Alan and Alison's feedback leaves Victoria reaching for the tissues

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Ye Old Crown in Edenbridge, Kent, where chef and owner Aaron Webster aims to deliver a three star service for a two star price

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Beach Lodge Hotel in Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, as hosts Cheryl and Michele aim to offer their guests a perfect seaside break

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Alison play host to the others at the Farmer's Arms in Taunton, where they hope their palatial rooms will help them win. But a dusty radiator might be their undoing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores, with Coaching Inn owner Victoria wanting to get to the bottom of Michele and Cheryl's comments about her toilet

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Jay and Charlene, parents to one-year-old Kai, who bought and refurbished their two bed home in Romford, Essex but now want to upsize to Chelmsford. Meanwhile, Phil Spencer assists Jemma and Graham, who returned from Los Angeles three years ago and want to settle in Southend-on-Sea

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the music quiz, with five players battling it out for a place in Original PopMaster. Only one can remain victorious and play 3 in 10. Will they score high enough to guarantee a place in the Grand Final?

programme.title: Panda
programme.desc: The detective is panicking about Sacha's first visit, and is persuaded to investigate the murder of a dance teacher at a senior citizens' village. He joins the class on the dance floor in a bid to uncover the residents' secrets, and very quickly, the suspicion of a blackmailer emerges. In French

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Two families come to terms with life-changing events, including the relatives of eight-year-old Junior, who has an open leg fracture and suspected internal bleeding after being knocked off his bike by a car. His mum Bekki reveals how Junior learned to cope with extraordinary setbacks and how she worries about his future because of health problems of her own. Meanwhile, 46-year-old Anthony arrives in A&E after suffering chest pains during a day out at the races. As doctors attempt to get to the bottom of what's causing his dangerously high heart rate, his wife discusses how Anthony's awareness of his mortality was increased by losing his father

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics are called out to a van and a car that have collided at a roundabout, involving a man who must always wear a crash helmet to protect himself from frequent seizures. A woman at a social club fears chest pains could be a sign of a heart attack. And a trip to the shops ends badly after a nasty fall

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A man with a deep cut to the head refuses to go to hospital for fear that he won't come out again. It's not often the paramedics treat a patient who runs off, but that's what happens when they're called to a suspected drug overdose. A homeless man is in agony with spasms in his back, and a 12-year-old girl with epilepsy has a seizure, but her mother still wants her to go to school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Two families come to terms with life-changing events, including the relatives of eight-year-old Junior, who has an open leg fracture and suspected internal bleeding after being knocked off his bike by a car. His mum Bekki reveals how Junior learned to cope with extraordinary setbacks and how she worries about his future because of health problems of her own. Meanwhile, 46-year-old Anthony arrives in A&E after suffering chest pains during a day out at the races. As doctors attempt to get to the bottom of what's causing his dangerously high heart rate, his wife discusses how Anthony's awareness of his mortality was increased by losing his father

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Greg, 47, is airlifted to St George's with critical injuries to his arm and leg after being knocked off his motorbike on the way into work. As his wife Lucie rushes to his bedside, she's shocked by what she finds. Cameras also follow, Cara, 11, who has fallen from the top of a human pyramid doing gymnastics and has a broken arm that doctors must try to realign

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: This week's campers - Simon and Emmy, Sean and Marie, and Jen, Ian and Ellis - arrive at Blair Castle Caravan Park at the edge of the Cairngorms National Park

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Shannon Slater-Dent and Trevor Tredgett's Bove Town House in Glastonbury. The duo are keen to show off what their B&B is all about, so lead an afternoon of group sound healing. While Ben Davenport and Sian Dewaal from the Fritton Arms immediately recognise that this B&B will be tough competition, Stevie King and his head chef Dave from SugarBeat Eating House dismiss the activity as being 'hippy'. However, it's the poor night's sleep that bothers Kathryn and her best friend Trevor, from Treberthan B&B, as well as the fact they have to cook their own breakfast. At feedback time, the idea of self B&B comes under fire and Kathryn doesn't hold back with her sleep score

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Treberthan B&B in Par, Cornwall, where owner Kathryn Langley and her best friend Trevor Burnell set out to give their guests a taste of Cornish hospitality. Everyone's impressed by the high standard of cleanliness, but Stevie and David are put off by the old-fashioned decor. A Cornish pasty making competition reveals Kathryn's assertive streak, while professional chef David feels under pressure to win, Ben and Sian make an unconventional pasty, and Shannon and Trevor forget a vital ingredient. At breakfast, a request for goat's milk puts Kathryn under pressure

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Stevie King and his head chef David Lovett host at SugarBeat Eating House in Swainsthorpe in Norfolk. The cleanliness comes under fire as Kathryn and Trevor struggle with their marked shower screen, and Ben and Sian write a message in the dust they find. Over dinner, Ben goes into detective mode to find out everything he can about his SugarBeat rivals. However, it's Kathryn's straight talking that becomes the talking point for Shannon and Trevor

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Fritton Arms in Norfolk, run by Ben Davenport and partner Sian Dewaal. David and Stevie are upset about their feedback so they conduct a forensic-style room inspection - complete with rubber gloves - and a paddleboat race on a nearby lake brings out everyone's competitive nature. Ben impresses the guests by revealing the hotel's aristocratic connections - but soon comes under fire for allowing pets to stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners reunite to find out what they have been paid, with Shannon and Trevor from Bove Town House wanting to discuss Kathryn's comments about her poor night's sleep

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson helps Gary and Jan find their dream holiday home in southwest France. Armed with a budget of £140,000 the group search the countryside of Haute-Vienne for a detached house with lots of outdoor space and a pool where they can spend several months of the year enjoying the great outdoors with their families

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ginny and Tony went on holiday to the Mani Peninsula in Greece four years ago, fell in love with the area and have since been back seven times. Their dream is to find the perfect property that they can retire to permanently. For their £230,000 budget, Laura Hamilton shows them five terrific options to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson goes house-hunting with retired NHS theatre nurse Linda and painter and decorator Peter around the countryside of Abruzzo in Italy. The couple love nature and spending time in the great outdoors on their canal boat, and can't wait to spend half the year in a dream holiday home. Jean sorts out five options for them to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sue and Pete cannot wait to fulfil their dream of moving to Cyprus and starting a whole new life in the sunshine with their five-month-old puppy Billy, in the region of Paphos, on the west coast of the island. They call on Jasmine Harman to help them find a detached house with a pool for their budget of £250,000

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In north Surrey, planning enforcement officer Becky is joined by singing teacher Petrina, drum teacher Rob, solicitor Shoba and building company boss Giovanni. Becky plans to delight her guests with an evening of modern home-cooking and a menu including butternut, lamb and a deconstructed apple dessert. The week gets off to an intriguing start with young gun Rob taking a shine to Petrina and vice versa

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Solicitor Shoba hosts the second night in north Surrey, and tries to impress with an Indian-themed menu. However, the guests are less than impressed with the food, especially the main course - a curry made from frozen fish - but will a bit of belly dancing, Bollywood-style, help save the day? Meanwhile, singer Petrina and drummer Rob continue their flirting both at and away from the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Drum tutor Rob hosts the third night in north Surrey, hoping his Thai-themed menu will impress his guests, as well as the object of his affections, Petrina. He serves prawns with lemon, lime and chilli for his starter, Thai chicken curry for his main, and rounds off with his own take on an ice-cream sundae. As a single man living in his stepmother's house, Rob thinks his competitors will see him as an underdog, so is intent on pulling out all the stops

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Singing teacher Petrina hosts the fourth dinner party in north Surrey, and she believes her experience of entertaining on cruise ships will give her the edge as she plans a showstopper of an evening. However, the diners seem more interested in their host's continued flirting with fellow guest Rob. Will tonight be the night, or will he shy away when he discovers Petrina has sung in the West End and worked with dozens of household names?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Giovanni hosts the final night of the competition in north Surrey, intent on flying the flag for Sicily with some family inspired recipes. Even with help from his father, a tagliatelle tragedy leaves him with Parmesan on his face. Becky is less than impressed with Giovanni's dishes, and matters go from bad to worse when he attempts to serve his home-made pasta. However, poor food doesn't deter a romantic Rob from popping the question to Petrina, sending shockwaves across the dining room. In a final attempt to bag the £1,000, Giovanni invites an opera singer to liven up the proceedings, but his musical initiative may prove too little to save the day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: This week kicks off at Na'im and Sara's Lovelady Shield Hotel in Cumbria. The views are welcomed by most of the B&guests - but one pair are preoccupied by the furniture

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's second visit comes from the Ryebeck Hotel in the Lake District, with glorious views of Lake Windermere. But it's other views left in the feedback that take centre stage

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers go off-grid at Dale2Swale Glamping in Richmond, North Yorkshire, but some guests are worried about how they'll survive the night without their creature comforts

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week takes the B&Bers to Ashbourne Holidays in Derbyshire, where deer wander around the grounds and the guests pay a visit to a local animal park

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On payment day, the B&Bers meet for the last time, and clash over furniture failings and clock complaints. Sparks fly - rather ironically - over a lack of electricity

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A patient undergoing treatment for cancer develops a swelling in her throat that doctors fear may be a side-effect of the revolutionary new procedure she has been undergoing. Drastic steps have to be taken to save a patient with a dangerously high heart rate, and a seven-year-old boy is brought in after injuring his chest in a golf buggy accident

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A woman is transferred to St George's after crashing her car into a tree, and has to have her broken ankle very quickly realigned if she is to have any hope of walking again. A man who collapsed suddenly is suspected of having a life-threatening head injury, while a four-year-old girl needs treatment after falling on an escalator

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A teenager has a life-threatening bleed on the brain and requires emergency surgery, a 10-year-old gymnast sustains an injury to her wrist after performing a back bend, and a girl is brought in to the urgent care centre, needing treatment for her swollen tonsils. As they undergo treatment, their parents share stories of family life

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A young man is found drunk and passed out in the street, pregnant woman is vomiting continuously and exhausted, while a man is in agony with what the paramedics think is gallstones. An elderly man falls over and badly cuts his head. He lives alone with help from carers and is fiercely independent, but his son is concerned this will happen again

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A patient undergoing treatment for cancer develops a swelling in her throat that doctors fear may be a side-effect of the revolutionary new procedure she has been undergoing. Drastic steps have to be taken to save a patient with a dangerously high heart rate, and a seven-year-old boy is brought in after injuring his chest in a golf buggy accident

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A woman is transferred to St George's after crashing her car into a tree, and has to have her broken ankle very quickly realigned if she is to have any hope of walking again. A man who collapsed suddenly is suspected of having a life-threatening head injury, while a four-year-old girl needs treatment after falling on an escalator

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Midway through their tour of the Scottish Highlands, Sean and Marie, Jen, Ian and Ellis, and Simon and Emmy pitch up at Ben Nevis Holiday Park, at the foot of the famous landmark

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A man with a deep cut to the head refuses to go to hospital for fear that he won't come out again. It's not often the paramedics treat a patient who runs off, but that's what happens when they're called to a suspected drug overdose. A homeless man is in agony with spasms in his back, and a 12-year-old girl with epilepsy has a seizure, but her mother still wants her to go to school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Brooke is rushed to St George's after suffering an asthma attack so severe that the paramedic team had to put her in an induced coma at the scene. Doctors are concerned that she may have dangerous amounts of fluid on her lungs and decide to take urgent chest X-rays. Howard, who's 76, arrives with burns to his face, hands and airways after trying to put out an explosion in his garden shed. As his worried wife waits for news in the relatives' room, doctors attempt to stabilise his condition and investigate the extent of his injuries

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A look at the specialist team dedicated to caring for people when they suffer the potentially devastating consequences of a stroke, including 77-year-old Alex. Husband Paul sits nervously at her bedside as they wait to see if the drug she has been given will bring back her speech and movement. Meanwhile, doctors examine 66-year-old Steven, who has been admitted with severe stomach cramps

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Three groups of campers explore The Highlands of Scotland. Their first stop is Aberfeldy Caravan Park on the river Tay, where a ceilidh and some whitewater rafting are on the cards

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: On the Wirral, Simon O'Brien has a crazy plan to turn a chunky chain into a standard lamp, while Henry Cole restores a 1963 Ford Anglia

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team is near Preston, where Simon O'Brien restores a spotlight he believes may be from a German U-boat while Henry Cole gets creative with an aircraft jump seat

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Claire Hilton, who runs Robertsbrook Guest House in Swanage, Dorset, feels she has a good chance of winning the title of best B&B as another round of the competition gets under way. However, guests Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome have doubts over the cleanliness of their bedroom, and another couple are accustomed to more opulent surroundings

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome hope to impress with their two-star B&B Eden Lodge in Falmouth, Cornwall. However, the state of their property disappoints their fellow competitors. At dinner, James and Maria Church reveal the truth about their famous daughter Charlotte, and the following morning's breakfast reduces some of the guests to fits of giggles

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It is the turn of Charlotte Church's parents James and Maria to play host at Dexby Townhouse in Cardiff, and they pull out all the stops by laying on a day of white-water rafting and a magic performance courtesy of their nephew Elliot. But the following morning, some of the guests are kept waiting for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Newlyweds Pip Easby and Nicky Lesser-Easby, who run Muddifords Court Country House in Devon, open their doors for the final stay of the competition. The Churches find their four-poster bed too small, and not all the guests enjoy their trip to a local glass-blowing factory, but feedback at check-out time leaves the hosts feeling confident

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. An underpayment from a surprising source changes the dynamics of the group and throws the competition wide open, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former property developer Ian hosts the first dinner party in Gloucestershire, hoping his super-healthy menu will win him the £1,000 prize - although it is possible his guests may be more impressed with his 12-bedroom mansion in the country. Some naked antics, a pinata and stories of millions pounds won and lost make it a night to remember

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Then-Miss Cheltenham Sophie hosts the second dinner party in Gloucestershire, hoping her evening of elegant food and pageantry will win her the £1,000 prize. The 24-year-old reveals what she does for a living to her guests, and one attendee makes a surprising confession of her own

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Rugby-loving Carmel hosts the third dinner party in Gloucestershire, and she hopes to impress with a 'Six Nations' starter, a pancetta, king prawn and chorizo main, and a Key lime pie for dessert. An attempt to charm her guests backfires when she likens Ian to a psychotic killer, and the atmosphere takes a further knock after Chris gets on the wrong side of Sophie

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: School teacher Polly, 40, hosts the fourth night of the competition in Gloucestershire, and hopes to impress her guests with a fine-dining experience. She begins with a seafood starter before serving up a meaty main course, and isn't unnerved by the fact she hasn't had any practise cooking either dish. However, her guests show off round the table and threaten to take the attention away from the night, but Ian is highly impressed with Polly's choice of entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the turn of exuberant hair salon owner Chris to host the final dinner party in Gloucestershire with home-made treats on the menu, including a tiger prawn starter, venison for the main and a dessert of chocolate and walnut ice-cream. Chat around the table reveals some differing opinions after the guests make a discovery in the host's living room, and the group reflect on their time together before the winner of the cash prize is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The latest competition begins in Norfolk at the Old Ram Coaching Inn, one of five-star-trained Victoria MacDonald's three B&B sites. Guests Michele and Cheryl aren't impressed with Victoria's dusty towel rail and a suspicious brown smudge in the toilet. However, Aaron and Mel aren't happy about a hairy bed. At breakfast, Aaron fumes over his eggs, and Alan and Alison's feedback leaves Victoria reaching for the tissues

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Ye Old Crown in Edenbridge, Kent, where chef and owner Aaron Webster aims to deliver a three star service for a two star price

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Beach Lodge Hotel in Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, as hosts Cheryl and Michele aim to offer their guests a perfect seaside break

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Alison play host to the others at the Farmer's Arms in Taunton, where they hope their palatial rooms will help them win. But a dusty radiator might be their undoing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores, with Coaching Inn owner Victoria wanting to get to the bottom of Michele and Cheryl's comments about her toilet

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Jay and Charlene, parents to one-year-old Kai, who bought and refurbished their two bed home in Romford, Essex but now want to upsize to Chelmsford. Meanwhile, Phil Spencer assists Jemma and Graham, who returned from Los Angeles three years ago and want to settle in Southend-on-Sea

programme.title: World's Greatest Train Journeys from Above

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation

programme.title: Seconds from Disaster

programme.title: Car S.O.S.

programme.title: Forgotten Allies: The Search for Burma's Lost Heroes
programme.desc: Documentary shining a light on the bravery of thousands of Burmese men who fought for Britain in the darkest hours of the Second World War. programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.desc: Dan Jones, Suzannah Lipscomb and Rob Bell take a fresh look at the Great Fire of London in 1666 by walking the actual route the fire took across the city, hour-by-hour and street-by-street, hoping to uncover what really happened during Britain's worst inferno. In this first edition, the three presenters investigate how the fire started, heading to Pudding Lane, site of the bakery where the blaze broke out. Dan and historian Dorian Gerhold then place a temporary 'blue plaque' on the very spot where the fire began

programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.desc: The team continues to walk the path of the infamous inferno, uncovering what really happened on the worst day of the four-day rampage. Dan Jones charts the destruction of the city's buildings, including London's spiritual home St Paul's Cathedral, which burned so fiercely its stones exploded. Suzannah Lipscomb reveals how Pudding Lane baker Thomas Farriner made sure no blame landed on him and Rob Bell finds out how the fire moved so fast

programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.desc: Documentary following the last day of the fire's rampage through the city of London in 1666, and the after-effects that changed the capital city for ever. The team investigate the treatment for burns victims, and how authorities dealt with the 100,000 refugees made homeless. Finally, they walk through some of the streets where the fire's impact still endures, and look at how London was rebuilt from the ashes

programme.title: Symbols of Evil
programme.desc: Exploring five examples of harmless symbols that have been turned into dangerous propaganda tools for movements that have hunted, tortured and killed, including the Hitler salute, the swastika, the Z of the Russian war against Ukraine, the Q of QAnon and the Star of David

programme.title: Heroes of the Somme
programme.desc: Military historian Dr Gavin Hughes traces the stories of seven of the men whose remarkable bravery won them the Victoria Cross, Britain's most prized military medal. programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. The body of an elderly woman is found in a cave, with her fingerprints burnt off and a posy of foxgloves at her side that she did not pick herself. The case is complicated further when they discover that she died three months before her body was discovered. Jack is apprehensive about the arrival of new boss Professor Harriet Maven (Maggie Steed)

programme.title: Anam: Songs for Hearts & Minds
programme.sub-title: Ormond Castle, Carrick On Suir, Co Tipperary
programme.desc: Musical performances set against the backdrop of Ireland's historic landmarks, kicking off in Tipperary's Ormond Castle, with songs from country and blues sensation Muireann Bradley, musical polymath Uly and a solo set by Big Thief's Adrianne Lenker

programme.title: Conversations with Friends
programme.desc: Frances writes her first story about her relationship with Bobbi, and discovers she has been cut off by her father. Her health continues to deteriorate, until she collapses. Bobbi sees how upset she is to be estranged from Nick, and invites him over - which leads to a shocking revelation

programme.title: Conversations with Friends
programme.desc: Melissa admits to Frances that things are better between her and Nick - and accepts that she is a reason for that, so therefore she'd like to find a way to make it all work. Frances receives a worrying message from her father, and Bobbi's unresolved issues concerning her parents' divorce cause her to lash out towards Philip's girlfriend

programme.title: Shortland Street
programme.desc: Esther blabs when she shouldn't, the cracks start to show for Drew, and Maeve questions her beliefs

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: David introduces Cash to the station's newest recruit - probationary constable Richard Brezniac. Roo presses Alf about booking his medical assessment

programme.title: Son of a Critch
programme.desc: Mark's excitement at seeing Halley's Comet is dampened when he hears Fox's family news

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey in the East Yorkshire resort of Hornsea, famous for its distinctive pottery. In Hull, he discovers how one of Hull FC's star players made history as Britain's first black rugby captain. From Hull, Michael hitches a ride on a fine-looking barge heading to Goole on a network of canals. At Spurn Point, Michael hears how along this coast an ingenious 19th-century RNLI invention saved many lives

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo has reached Lincolnshire, where his coastal railway journey from Middlesbrough ends. In Grimsby Town, he investigates a new stage in the town's history - as a maintenance base for some of the largest wind farms in the world. In neighbouring Cleethorpes, Michael rides on the Cleethorpes Coast Light Railway, pulled by a miniature steam locomotive. Later, Michael makes for a curious and wild stretch of coastline at Humberston

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Brad Bestelink witnesses Xudum the leopard fight to save her newborn cubs, Anna Dimitriadis finds a brave cheetah thriving in the heart of lion territory, and Vianet Djenguet tracks lioness Tsebe as she risks her life to feed her five cubs. As the lion pride continues to grow, becoming the largest in Africa, competition for food leads to chaos and infighting. Could this pride's success also be it downfall?

programme.title: Oasis: Supersonic (2016)
programme.desc: Documentary about the early years of the Manchester band who dominated the charts in the mid-1990s. The film focuses on the relationship between Gallagher brothers Noel and Liam, including their infamous sibling rivalry, and also features rare concert footage. With Noel Gallagher, Liam Gallagher, Peggie Gallagher, Paul 'Bonehead' Arthurs, Paul 'Guigsy' McGuigan, Tony McCarroll, Alan McGee and Owen Morris

programme.title: Operation Mincemeat (2021)
programme.desc: During the Second World War, two intelligence officers use a corpse and false papers to outwit German troops. Director John Madden's drama, starring Colin Firth, Matthew Macfadyen and Kelly Macdonald

programme.title: The Rumour
programme.desc: Joanna reels from the truth while Bea faces the fallout. As Michael warns Bea, unaware they're being watched, Joanna discovers Rachel has taken Alfie. Desperate, she tracks them to a derelict house, where Rachel demands Bea's confession. Flames erupt, chaos ensues, and Joanna seizes the moment to escape with Alfie

programme.title: news2day Weekly
programme.desc: The stories making the headlines both at home and abroad during the past seven days

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mackenzie blows up at David, Levi fights to save a life, and Harper gets an unexpected message

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Cash reproaches Mackenzie for her outburst at David, and Tane's oxygen levels are plummeting, Levi and Jo are stumped on the cause of his worsening condition

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Dana shares the news with John over the arrest of her mother, and Justin reminds Lacey that her grief is still fresh and to give it time

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: A team of family and friends, Amy, JP, Nic and Alan, take on the Bridge of Lies, they must cross the Bridge, stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies. Hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Amanda & Alan's Italian Job
programme.desc: Amanda and Alan face their biggest challenge yet as they tackle the garden. The patch of land on the side of the mountain has been untouched for over a decade, and with no idea on what plants thrive in the Tuscan terrain, the pals seek help from gardening oracle Warrie Price, who has spent over 30 years cultivating a 15-acre oasis. Keen to inject authentic Tuscan design, Alan visits the ancient town of Impruneta in search of terracotta pots. Amanda calls in her green-fingered mum, but gets more than she bargained for when her parent takes a liking to a member of staff at a nearby garden centre

programme.title: Amanda & Alan's Italian Job
programme.desc: Alan Carr takes charge tackling the first bedroom, and inspired by the romance and history of his favourite city Florence, he is determined to have a mural. However, co-renovator Amanda Holden fears his creative juices could turn into a flood. To nail his aesthetic, Alan travels to the outskirts of the Tuscan capital to meet a woman whose medieval home is decorated in rare 14th-century frescoes, and is encouraged to take down the ceiling to reveal any original features. After realising the existing furniture is riddled with woodworm, the duo head to the Gulf of Poets for inspiration

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets adventurous couple Colin and Adele. They have employed a Swedish architect, an Italian interior design company and a Latvian building company to complete the eye-popping curved glass family home in the Manchester suburb where they both grew up

programme.title: Champions League Live
programme.desc: Paris Saint-Germain v Arsenal (Kick-off 5.00pm). Darragh Maloney presents coverage of the final from Puskas Arena in Budapest. The Gunners made it to their first final in this competition since 2006 thanks to a 2-1 semi-final victory over Atlético Madrid, with Bukayo Saka scoring the winner in the second leg at the Emirates. PSG beat Bayern Munich 6-5 across two epic encounters to reach their second successive Champions League final. The French club are looking to become the first back-to-back winners of the competition since Real Madrid won three titles in a row between 2016 and 2018. With analysis from Kevin Doyle and Richie Sadlier

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look into the lives of mammals, exploring how they have adapted to their changing surroundings and have found ways to create homes in the wild. The first episode focuses on animals that thrive in the dark, from leopards that use night vision to hunt their prey to bats using echolocation to navigate the night skies

programme.title: D-Day: 100 Days for Freedom
programme.desc: An examination of the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. It was a period that made history, both heroic and tragic. The first of two programmes retraces events, day by day, from D-Day up to the Liberation of Paris, through a series of testimonies and colourised archives

programme.title: The Saturday Game
programme.desc: Action from the All-Ireland Senior Championship first-round matches, featuring Westmeath v Cavan at TEG Cusack Park and Armagh v Derry at BOX-IT Athletic Grounds

programme.title: Trump's Power & the Rule of Law
programme.desc: The high stakes showdown between President Donald Trump and the US courts over presidential power. Featuring insight from Trump allies, opponents and experts on how he tests the extent of his power, the legal pushback and impact on the rule of law

programme.title: The Rumour
programme.desc: Joanna reels from the truth while Bea faces the fallout. As Michael warns Bea, unaware they're being watched, Joanna discovers Rachel has taken Alfie. Desperate, she tracks them to a derelict house, where Rachel demands Bea's confession. Flames erupt, chaos ensues, and Joanna seizes the moment to escape with Alfie

programme.title: Tsunami: The Wave That Shook the World
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary recounting catastrophic events that unfolded during the morning of Boxing Day 2004 around the Indian Ocean coasts

programme.desc: European and international headlines live via satellite

programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey in the East Yorkshire resort of Hornsea, famous for its distinctive pottery. In Hull, he discovers how one of Hull FC's star players made history as Britain's first black rugby captain. From Hull, Michael hitches a ride on a fine-looking barge heading to Goole on a network of canals. At Spurn Point, Michael hears how along this coast an ingenious 19th-century RNLI invention saved many lives

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo has reached Lincolnshire, where his coastal railway journey from Middlesbrough ends. In Grimsby Town, he investigates a new stage in the town's history - as a maintenance base for some of the largest wind farms in the world. In neighbouring Cleethorpes, Michael rides on the Cleethorpes Coast Light Railway, pulled by a miniature steam locomotive. Later, Michael makes for a curious and wild stretch of coastline at Humberston

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7

As the lion pride continues to grow, becoming the largest in Africa, competition for food leads to chaos and infighting. Could this pride's success also be it downfall?

programme.title: Amanda & Alan's Italian Job
programme.desc: Amanda and Alan face their biggest challenge yet as they tackle the garden. The patch of land on the side of the mountain has been untouched for over a decade, and with no idea on what plants thrive in the Tuscan terrain, the pals seek help from gardening oracle Warrie Price, who has spent over 30 years cultivating a 15-acre oasis. Keen to inject authentic Tuscan design, Alan visits the ancient town of Impruneta in search of terracotta pots. Amanda calls in her green-fingered mum, but gets more than she bargained for when her parent takes a liking to a member of staff at a nearby garden centre

programme.title: Amanda & Alan's Italian Job
programme.desc: Alan Carr takes charge tackling the first bedroom, and inspired by the romance and history of his favourite city Florence, he is determined to have a mural. However, co-renovator Amanda Holden fears his creative juices could turn into a flood. To nail his aesthetic, Alan travels to the outskirts of the Tuscan capital to meet a woman whose medieval home is decorated in rare 14th-century frescoes, and is encouraged to take down the ceiling to reveal any original features. After realising the existing furniture is riddled with woodworm, the duo head to the Gulf of Poets for inspiration

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets adventurous couple Colin and Adele. They have employed a Swedish architect, an Italian interior design company and a Latvian building company to complete the eye-popping curved glass family home in the Manchester suburb where they both grew up

programme.title: Champions League Live
programme.desc: Paris Saint-Germain v Arsenal (Kick-off 5.00pm). Darragh Maloney presents coverage of the final from Puskas Arena in Budapest. The Gunners made it to their first final in this competition since 2006 thanks to a 2-1 semi-final victory over Atlético Madrid, with Bukayo Saka scoring the winner in the second leg at the Emirates. PSG beat Bayern Munich 6-5 across two epic encounters to reach their second successive Champions League final. The French club are looking to become the first back-to-back winners of the competition since Real Madrid won three titles in a row between 2016 and 2018. With analysis from Kevin Doyle and Richie Sadlier

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look into the lives of mammals, exploring how they have adapted to their changing surroundings and have found ways to create homes in the wild. The first episode focuses on animals that thrive in the dark, from leopards that use night vision to hunt their prey to bats using echolocation to navigate the night skies

programme.title: D-Day: 100 Days for Freedom
programme.desc: An examination of the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. It was a period that made history, both heroic and tragic. The first of two programmes retraces events, day by day, from D-Day up to the Liberation of Paris, through a series of testimonies and colourised archives

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: The team investigate the killing of an Asian shopkeeper who tried to forbid dope dealers from operating outside his store. His killing leads detectives to Mahoney, whose parting shot to Kellerman throws the detective into a tailspin of depression

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street

programme.title: Succession

programme.title: House of the Dragon
