programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's journey takes him to the Spanish border and the Mediterranean coast. He discovers how Catalan and French influences have shaped the culinary legacy of Roussillon in the foothills of the Pyrenees, and samples some of the area's famous mountain cuisine, including grilled sausages, snails and fresh fish caught in a self-contained seawater lake - all with generous servings of the region's famous garlic mayonnaise. Back in his country kitchen, Rick prepares pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Adrian Edmondson, Claude Littner, Cariad Lloyd and Jordan Stephens to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actress and writer Daisy May Cooper, quiz guru Richard Osman, actress, comedian and writer Diane Morgan, and journalist and broadcaster Rory Reid join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kemah Bob, JJ Chalmers, Kevin Clifton and Victoria Derbyshire test their general knowledge skills in the hope of winning a prize, with the week's overall winner being decided

programme.title: The Lakes with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the beautiful and unique landscapes of the Lake District, beginning by meeting Cumbrians with different visions for the future of England's biggest national park. He talks to members of a campaign group trying to protect the remaining red squirrels, a traditional farmer who feels that his ancient way of life is on the point of extinction, and an 18-year-old student who runs a 1,500-acre farm

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson finds a novel way to drive the Vauxhall Signum, General Motors' revolutionary Hy-wire concept car gets a test-drive and there's a look at Volkswagen's luxury limo the Phaeton. Actor Patrick Stewart gets up to warp speed on the racetrack as the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson climbs aboard a Bellagio Land Rover and takes a spin in a Hartage Mini. James May gets passionate over the Cadillac 16, and Richard Hammond tests out an automobile which travels on land and water. Plus, advances in vehicle safety and the results of the vote for the world's greatest car. Actor Alan Davies is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the links between American and British culinary cultures. They make clam chowder and chocolate brownies, before visiting London's Savoy hotel to discuss the history of the cocktail and reveal how the farmers' market is one of the UK's favourite imports from the United States

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's journey takes him to the Spanish border and the Mediterranean coast. He discovers how Catalan and French influences have shaped the culinary legacy of Roussillon in the foothills of the Pyrenees, and samples some of the area's famous mountain cuisine, including grilled sausages, snails and fresh fish caught in a self-contained seawater lake - all with generous servings of the region's famous garlic mayonnaise. Back in his country kitchen, Rick prepares pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Adrian Edmondson, Claude Littner, Cariad Lloyd and Jordan Stephens to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Celebrity guests Fern Britton, Richard E Grant, Sanjeev Bhaskar and Martin Clunes join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the comedy panel show in which contestants try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon presents

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kevin Eldon, Sarah Keyworth, Laila Rouass and John Whaite take part in the final challenges before the week's victor is announced by Richard Osman

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig and Alan Davies are joined by Josh Widdicombe, Susan Calman and Matt Lucas for this extended edition of the long-running panel show - and this week it's a festive special, so Sandi's elves have been busy coming up with tough questions on the subject of - what else? - Noel. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers considered to be most interesting

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Charlie Brooker hosts an extended episode of the satirical current affairs quiz, with team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton joined by Grayson Perry and Angela Barnes

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy quiz, with team captains Rob Beckett and Aisling Bea joined by guests Jamali Maddix, Rachel Riley, Sara Pascoe and Tom Davis

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Tom Allen, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman, Rhys James and Rosie Jones and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Social niceties are stretched to snapping point when Lee and Lucy take in a neighbour's delivery for safekeeping. Lee unknowingly pulls apart the packaging, only to discover it's a somewhat personal item. To top it off, there's no way to hide the fact it's been opened. Thus, getting it back to its rightful owner proves easier said than done. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Hugh Dennis

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: To celebrate their anniversary, Lee and Lucy plan a friendly night of quizzing with Toby and Anna in their local pub. However, the evening is derailed when neither couple can bear to lose to their partners and the foursome splits into rival teams. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Hugh Dennis

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Adrian Edmondson, Claude Littner, Cariad Lloyd and Jordan Stephens to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: The crew members need urgent replacement engine parts, which can only be obtained if Lister marries the hideous daughter of an alien tribal chief. Sci-fi comedy, starring Chris Barrie, Craig Charles, Robert Llewellyn and Danny John-Jules

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Rimmer abandons his crewmates on a disintegrating spaceship, and ends up marooned on a barren planet for 600 years. After decades of boredom, he uses the escape pod's cloning equipment to populate his new home with duplicates of himself. Sci-fi comedy, starring Chris Barrie

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: An identical ship materialises alongside Starbug - and the crew members are alarmed to receive an SOS call from themselves, 15 years in the future. Sci-fi comedy, starring Chris Barrie, Craig Charles, Robert Llewellyn and Danny John-Jules

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Tom creates a new drink and invites everyone to visit the Snakehole to try it out. Ann is encouraged to apply for a city job, but then falls out with Leslie over their dating habits. Comedy, starring Amy Poehler, Aziz Ansari, Rashida Jones and Nick Offerman

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: US host Sean Kelly oversees UK bidders battling to win potential treasures found in storage containers, lock-ups and garages in Ipswich

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by actors Kevin Bishop and Brian Blessed, comedian Josh Widdicombe and historian Kate Williams, who aim to help the team captains deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc traps the teams in an off-the-scale world where nothing is as it seems, forcing them to decipher distorted clues and tackle a ticking time bomb.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guest players Rhod Gilbert, Jamie Laing, Tracy-Ann Oberman and Claudia Winkleman, who aim to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by Radio 1 DJ Clara Amfo, actor Tom Davis, actor and musician Shaun Ryder and comedian Henning Wehn, aiming to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by space scientist Maggie Aderin-Pocock, comedian Phil Wang, actor Jason Watkins and EastEnders star Adam Woodyatt, aiming to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: The crew members need urgent replacement engine parts, which can only be obtained if Lister marries the hideous daughter of an alien tribal chief. Sci-fi comedy, starring Chris Barrie, Craig Charles, Robert Llewellyn and Danny John-Jules

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Rimmer abandons his crewmates on a disintegrating spaceship, and ends up marooned on a barren planet for 600 years. After decades of boredom, he uses the escape pod's cloning equipment to populate his new home with duplicates of himself. Sci-fi comedy, starring Chris Barrie

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: An identical ship materialises alongside Starbug - and the crew members are alarmed to receive an SOS call from themselves, 15 years in the future. Sci-fi comedy, starring Chris Barrie, Craig Charles, Robert Llewellyn and Danny John-Jules

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Tom Allen, Angela Barnes, Rhys James, Nigel Ng and Athena Kugblenu. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Social niceties are stretched to snapping point when Lee and Lucy take in a neighbour's delivery for safekeeping. Lee unknowingly pulls apart the packaging, only to discover it's a somewhat personal item. To top it off, there's no way to hide the fact it's been opened. Thus, getting it back to its rightful owner proves easier said than done. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Hugh Dennis

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: To celebrate their anniversary, Lee and Lucy plan a friendly night of quizzing with Toby and Anna in their local pub. However, the evening is derailed when neither couple can bear to lose to their partners and the foursome splits into rival teams. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Hugh Dennis

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Jo Brand hosts an extended episode of the satirical current affairs quiz, with team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton joined by comedian Nabil Abdulrashid and journalist Tom Peck

programme.title: Mel Giedroyc: Unforgivable
programme.desc: Mel and Lou are joined by Alex Jones, Iain Stirling and Tim Renkow as Alex reveals how she got into trouble with the BBC and Iain spills the beans on being sacked

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Chris sends Leslie and Ben on a road trip to Indianapolis, and Ron teaches a young student about his views on the government. Meanwhile, Andy gets his feelings hurt by April after playing Tom's new game. Comedy, starring Amy Poehler, Aziz Ansari, Rashida Jones and Nick Offerman

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer is arrested on suspicion of armed robbery - and to make matters worse, the only way he can clear his name is by admitting he was with a charming ex-girlfriend at the time. He must decide whether to come clean and face a jealous Charlotte's wrath, or take the consequences of the charge. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: While a visiting theatrical troupe puts on a nativity play at St. Judas, a precious holy relic goes missing, and the actor playing Jesus is killed

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A softer side of Smith emerges after he helps deliver a baby. Alex Walkinshaw stars

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi is relieved to find he has someone to confide in when Ronnie tells him he knows the truth about Eva. Mike makes an effort with Michelle, but the couple break up. Vivien asks for help preparing for her forthcoming court case, and George helps a man make a better job of caring for his cancer-stricken wife

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: When a pretty student wakes to find herself in the class geek's bed, it sparks a day from hell which ends at the Campus Surgery

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Zubin gives evidence at the inquest into Dominic Fryer's death, where his personal conflicts with Ric are called into question and he is left reconsidering his future. However, on his return to the hospital he discovers a potentially lethal mishap with the oxygen supply and has to race against time to save a patient's life. News of Diane's pregnancy spreads across the wards, but she faces a difficult choice when Connie offers her a prestigious research position. Chrissie warns Owen the baby changes nothing - his relationship is still a terrible mistake

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Dixie is forced to take a call during her break, only to become frustrated by what appears to be a hoax. Unhappy with her working conditions, she complains to ambulance controller Louise, but their feud has fatal consequences. Meanwhile, Dylan goes beyond the call of duty to help a frightened youngster whose mother appears to have taken an instant dislike to him

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A softer side of Smith emerges after he helps deliver a baby. Alex Walkinshaw stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: A vengeful Janine wreaks havoc for Bianca and Ronnie. Peggy is left devastated by Sam's shocking announcement, while Billy enlists Jay in his hunt for Phil

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Becca reveals her true colours in a shocking showdown with Stacey. Peggy tracks Phil down and is horrified by what she discovers. Ryan has a romantic surprise for Janine

programme.title: Harry Wild
programme.desc: A mystery novelist is found dead in a locked panic room with no gun. Private eyes Fergus and Harry investigate the peculiar case while fending off the meddlesome Harry Benedict

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: A hotel owner finds a body in one of the rooms and Blake's investigation leads him to a woman in Mattie's care who has lost her memory after a car crash

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A penniless sculptor seeks the canny dealer's help to prise a considerable inheritance out of his avaricious brother. Celia Imrie guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: The staff at Bayview Retirement Village are sent into a panic when Diana and Tom head off to Brighton for the day in a borrowed Porshe without telling anyone. Comedy, starring Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo is stunned to catch Nora Batty in a church hall closet with Billy Chiselhurst. Realising he has a rival for her affections, he challenges Billy to a duel at dawn. Bill Owen, Kathy Staff and John Jardine star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler comes face to face with the woman of his dreams and promptly sweeps her off her feet - on to the back of his tandem, and Barry resolves to throw caution to the wind and show his affection for Glenda. Roy Clarke's long-running comedy, starring Jean Alexander, Thora Hird and Stephen Lewis alongside stalwarts Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy encounters an unlucky chimney sweep, and sets out to find him a good old-fashioned chimney to sweep intent on helping preserve the tradition. Comedy, starring Brain Wilde

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Lady Felicia's home comes under threat, Father Brown is forced to seek help from an unlikely ally. Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll star

programme.title: Happy Valley
programme.desc: Sgt Catherine Cawood discovers the remains of a gangland murder victim in a drained reservoir, sparking a chain of events that leads straight back to Tommy Lee Royce. Catherine's grandson Ryan is now 16 and has his own ideas about the kind of relationship he wants to have with the man Catherine refuses to acknowledge as the boy's father. Meanwhile, in another part of town a local pharmacist gets in over his head when a neighbour is arrested. Crime drama, starring Sarah Lancashire, Siobhan Finneran and James Norton

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The body of a missing computer expert turns up in a hospital morgue under a false name, so the team reopens the investigation into his disappearance. The dead man worked for the Metropolitan Police, giving him access to a great deal of sensitive information - added to which, he was in contact with a group of online hackers. But he died of natural causes - so why was his body hidden, and by who? As the seasoned detectives unravel the mystery, Brian struggles to accept Steve as the new Ucos member, forcing Esther to intervene. Guest starring Sarah Smart and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan as the chalk-and-cheese detectives. The mysterious death of a wealthy entrepreneur opens up a hornet's nest of family tensions, betrayal and cover-ups involving radioactive waste - and brings Dalziel face to face with an old flame. Cherie Lunghi guest stars

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A penniless sculptor seeks the canny dealer's help to prise a considerable inheritance out of his avaricious brother. Celia Imrie guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Harry Wild
programme.sub-title: You Are Cordially Invited to an Assassination: Part 2
programme.desc: With the lives of people they care for in the balance, Harry and Fergus call on all the help they can to try and stop the assassin

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kingdom
programme.desc: Polish concentration camp survivor John Narbutowicz (Joss Ackland) seeks Peter's help when the council insists on entering his home to clear it of rubbish. Beatrice hopes to bond with Gloria and ropes her in on a girls' day out. Meanwhile, Snell has a dream that sets him off on an adventure, and Mrs Thing is persuaded to leave her money to the church. Rory Bremner guest stars as the local vicar

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The police use Sharon's house for a surveillance operation - prompting a debate about who they might be watching. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon's growing relationship with boyfriend Colin hits the rocks, threatening her new-found happiness. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien has a life-changing experience with a psychic, while Tracey takes charge of a boys' football team. Starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A friend who runs a fairground asks Lovejoy to sell silver candlesticks on his behalf, but the police arrive and reveal they are stolen property. Jimmy Jewel and Phil Daniels guest star, with Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.sub-title: Never Judge a Book by Its Cover
programme.desc: While searching a Cambridge antiques fair for religious books with Charlotte and Charlie Gimbert, Lovejoy discovers a rare Bible that seems to offer a solution to all his problems. Meanwhile, Tinker has a hard time persuading Beth's father that she should carry on working for such a rogue. Drama, guest starring Richard Briers and James Ellis, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A romantic weekend in the country turns sour for Charlotte and Lovejoy when one of her friends takes a shine to him. Matters deteriorate further for the dodgy dealer when he learns Charlie is trying to evict his business. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane, Caroline Langrishe and Malcolm Tierney

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Lady Felicia's home comes under threat, Father Brown is forced to seek help from an unlikely ally. Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: In a special edition of the detective drama that features only two members of the team, Gerry Standing and Steve McAndrew travel to Glasgow, where a new Ucos section is being set up. While there, they agree to assist the investigation into the 1993 unsolved murder of James Soutar, the wealthy owner of a string of betting shops. Cathy Sinclair, one of the beneficiaries of the bookie's will at his death, had a care-home upbringing in common with Soutar, but she claims to have no idea who he was or why she was left £15,000. McAndrew has a personal stake in the case - the original investigating officer was corrupt cop Frank McNair, who had an affair with his wife, and their paths soon cross. The London-based officers' questions begin to unsettle someone and a campaign of victimisation against them begins, but it's a revelation from McAndrew's girlfriend Charley that reawakens a long-dormant scandal and clears the path to a solution. Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Strickland's Whitehall pal Stephen Fisher is targeted by an assassin, so the Ucos boss enlists the team to find whoever was responsible. He reveals that 30 years earlier, he and Fisher were involved in a covert MI5 operation to break into the house of a journalist believed to have information on an IRA arms deals. But with several of the original agents having died in suspicious circumstances, he believes someone is out to kill them all - and when a connection is made between a gangster and MI5, it seems the threat may be coming from within the security service itself. Tim McInnerny and Kerry Fox guest star

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: An unpopular colonel is found dead in the vicarage of St Mary Mead and investigations reveal no shortage of suspects - including the vicar and members of the victim's family. Christmas 1986 edition of the whodunit, starring Joan Hickson, with Paul Eddington and James Hazeldine

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: After a man is fatally stabbed during a séance, and no weapon is found, Shepherd digs into the eerie incident to catch the murderer; questioning if supernatural forces were involved, the team must separate superstition from reality

programme.title: Monsieur Spade
programme.desc: Spade interrogates the intruder and learns that there are people who still very much want him dead. Another body is discovered, and Teresa begins her own investigation

programme.title: The Last Detective
programme.desc: Davies is saddened by the death of a harmless local tramp known for his penchant for collecting waste paper in a pram. The detective's instincts, along with the intervention of social services officer Jemma Duval, lead him to pursue the case, uncovering a wartime mystery along the way. Norman Wisdom stars alongside Peter Davison and Sean Hughes

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: In a special edition of the detective drama that features only two members of the team, Gerry Standing and Steve McAndrew travel to Glasgow, where a new Ucos section is being set up. While there, they agree to assist the investigation into the 1993 unsolved murder of James Soutar, the wealthy owner of a string of betting shops. Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Shelagh organises a ballroom dance to raise money for the maternity home, in the hope of taking her mind off May's impending adoption. A terminally ill patient laments that she will not get the chance to dance one last time, but Sister Hilda is determined to make sure she does. Fred suffers an embarrassing condition, and cannot bring himself to see Dr Turner, while Nurse Crane is discharged from hospital and tries to play matchmaker between Sgt Woolf and Miss Higgins. Valerie and Trixie are called to testify in a court case

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: The United Britain beauty contest comes to Slaughter Abbey, and Miss Northern Ireland - one of the three favourites - is found dead in the swimming pool. Sister Boniface deduces murder from a blow to the back of the head. Drama, starring Lorna Watson

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Shepherd and his team investigate when Peggy Froome, a devoted fan of the '80s band Stolen Arrow, falls to her death from a bell tower. The detective must dig into the band's secrets and dark history to find the actual cause of the victim's demise. Neill Rea stars

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Luftwaffe's bombing raids on Britain's provincial cities, when Coventry and Plymouth were hardest hit, although Portsmouth, Glasgow, Sheffield and Bristol also suffered heavy casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the morale of the Third Reich, boosted by Germany's initial military successes, was shattered by the devastating defeat at Stalingrad, relentless Allied bombing raids and an attempt on Hitler's life. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Churchill's plan to attack Germany through Italy. Although considered the simplest military solution, the Allies were to face formidable opposition, while the Americans met the Wehrmacht for the first time. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Luftwaffe's bombing raids on Britain's provincial cities, when Coventry and Plymouth were hardest hit, although Portsmouth, Glasgow, Sheffield and Bristol also suffered heavy casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher walks an eight-mile route along the River Lea, taking in the landmarks of an industrial past and discovering what the future holds for the waterway in this fast-growing part of London

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve continues his quest to trace the southern tropic, travelling from South Africa to Madagascar. In Zimbabwe, the presenter comes face to face with the harsh realities of the refugee crisis, and witnesses a unique method of land-mine disposal in Mozambique. Heading to the Madagascan islands, he discovers an unusual local delicacy, and visits a town where the poor make a living by digging for sapphires

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve embarks on the third leg of his journey, crossing the vast expanse of mainland Australia. Heading east from Ningaloo Reef, the explorer comes across a ghost town in Wittenoom Gorge, contaminated beyond habitation by a nearby asbestos mine. Travelling across the Northern Territory, Simon witnesses the shocking living conditions endured by Aboriginal communities in the shadow of Uluru

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey around the world, Simon Reeve follows the tropic across South America. In Argentina he meets an English anthropologist who married into a forest tribe. On the Paraguay-Brazil border he visits a hotbed of smuggling activity, and in Sao Paulo, the globetrotter takes in a neighbourhood formerly considered the most dangerous in the world

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon traces the path of the northern tropic around the globe, a journey in which he explores the environment, culture, history, politics and people of 18 countries. He begins in Baja California on Mexico's Pacific coast, crossing the country's rugged interior, before heading east to Cuba and ending the first leg in the Bahamas

programme.desc: How the morale of the Third Reich, boosted by Germany's initial military successes, was shattered by the devastating defeat at Stalingrad, relentless Allied bombing raids and an attempt on Hitler's life. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Churchill's plan to attack Germany through Italy. Although considered the simplest military solution, the Allies were to face formidable opposition, while the Americans met the Wehrmacht for the first time. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Luftwaffe's bombing raids on Britain's provincial cities, when Coventry and Plymouth were hardest hit, although Portsmouth, Glasgow, Sheffield and Bristol also suffered heavy casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher walks an eight-mile route along the River Lea, taking in the landmarks of an industrial past and discovering what the future holds for the waterway in this fast-growing part of London

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve continues his quest to trace the southern tropic, travelling from South Africa to Madagascar. In Zimbabwe, the presenter comes face to face with the harsh realities of the refugee crisis, and witnesses a unique method of land-mine disposal in Mozambique. Heading to the Madagascan islands, he discovers an unusual local delicacy, and visits a town where the poor make a living by digging for sapphires

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve embarks on the third leg of his journey, crossing the vast expanse of mainland Australia. Heading east from Ningaloo Reef, the explorer comes across a ghost town in Wittenoom Gorge, contaminated beyond habitation by a nearby asbestos mine. Travelling across the Northern Territory, Simon witnesses the shocking living conditions endured by Aboriginal communities in the shadow of Uluru

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey around the world, Simon Reeve follows the tropic across South America. In Argentina he meets an English anthropologist who married into a forest tribe. On the Paraguay-Brazil border he visits a hotbed of smuggling activity, and in Sao Paulo, the globetrotter takes in a neighbourhood formerly considered the most dangerous in the world

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon traces the path of the northern tropic around the globe, a journey in which he explores the environment, culture, history, politics and people of 18 countries. He begins in Baja California on Mexico's Pacific coast, crossing the country's rugged interior, before heading east to Cuba and ending the first leg in the Bahamas

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Built to shelter ancient China from hostile barbarians to the north, the Great Wall also serves as a cultural curtain, beyond which can be found a variety of traditions. This programme goes outside the Wall to meet tribes of reindeer herders on the Siberian border, and explores deserts to the north where ruined cities were once bustling staging posts for camel trains on the legendary Silk Road

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.sub-title: How to Ignite Your Errand Boy
programme.desc: Arkwright decides to make his own product, unhappy at the amount of profit lost to middlemen, and instructs Granville to come up with a suitable idea for something that is in daily demand, yet cheap and simple to make. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. Comedy, starring David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Sue Holderness

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Guy's constant presence in the flat continues to rile Lee and and his distrust is fuelled further when he discovers his unwanted housemate runs a lap-dancing club. Elsewhere, Tim's new relationship is going well until he discovers what his girlfriend does for a living. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Tim Vine

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The new boss makes his presence felt in the holiday camp with a spate of sackings. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane, David Griffin and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Don't Wait Up
programme.desc: Toby returns to his estranged wife, leaving Tom questioning his motives

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike discover their neighbour has been scheming behind their backs - his outlandish tactics threatening the success of their guesthouse business. For Alison, this warrants all out war and she sets about playing him at his own game. If she is going to stoop to such trickery however, she'll need the help of one of the ghosts. Mike attempts to mix things up for future guests at the gatehouse by secretly investing in some not-so-cheap entertainment that Alison will definitely not approve of. Meanwhile, the Captain makes a discovery, leading the other ghosts to find a surprising new friend

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Kevin and Perry try to befriend some girls at a party, while Tim Nice-But-Dim transforms himself into a rock star, and Lance sinks into depression when his imaginary girlfriend dumps him. Comedy sketches, featuring Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke

programme.desc: The employees are insulted by a suggestion that they take a wage cut, and decide to hold a rooftop demonstration. Comedy, starring Mollie Sugden and John Inman

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: A squeak on Granville's bike interferes with his hopes of romance, and Arkwright worries about a mysterious visitor to a house in Atkinson Terrace

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike and Alison welcome the very first guests to their new bijoux B&B. While serving the couple breakfast, Alison realises that they are not enjoying their break and, fearing a bad review, utilises the surprising skills of Lady Button and Julian to ensure the pair make the most of their weekend away. Thomas is perplexed yet secretly thrilled by the sudden attention from the Plaguers, who, after admiring Alison's discarded portrait of him are now affording him celebrity status in Button House, hanging on to his every word. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike discover their neighbour has been scheming behind their backs - his outlandish tactics threatening the success of their guesthouse business. For Alison, this warrants all out war and she sets about playing him at his own game. If she is going to stoop to such trickery however, she'll need the help of one of the ghosts. Mike attempts to mix things up for future guests at the gatehouse by secretly investing in some not-so-cheap entertainment that Alison will definitely not approve of. Meanwhile, the Captain makes a discovery, leading the other ghosts to find a surprising new friend

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Return of the comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby. Alison and Mike are trying to find a semi-harmonious routine with the ghostly housemates of Button House. The ghosts have found a way to make their new situation work for them, while the couple are working toward a new money-making venture to fund their perpetual renovations. But after a paranormal photo of the house goes viral, Alison and Mike spot an opportunity to bring money in more quickly by using the ghosts' 'talents' to create an authentic haunted-house experience

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: After a heavy night partying, Alison and Mike wake to find their house in disarray and with the clock ticking until a potentially crucial business partner comes to view the venue, the couple attempt a mass clean up. The ghosts are all acting very strangely, and Alison struggles to piece together the events of the night before to figure out what happened to them

programme.title: Wagspiracy: Vardy v Rooney
programme.desc: With access to key individuals who didn't give evidence at the trial, this documentary explores the hidden workings of the tabloids, PRs and agents connected to so-called 'Wagatha Christie', which followed accuisations between footballers' wives Coleen Rooney and Rebekah Vardy. Contributors include Colin Paterson and Lizzie Cundy

programme.title: Aaron Hernandez Uncovered
programme.desc: A detailed look at the life of Aaron Hernandez, a millionaire athlete with a fiancee and baby, who was shockingly convicted of murder

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Canadian comedian, actor and writer, Katherine Ryan, for a frank and honest conversation about her career, relationships and the changing shape of her family. Louis accompanies Katherine as she performs her latest stand-up tour, Missus, in Southend-On-Sea, and later, he spends a rare day off with Katherine, her daughter Violet and her husband Bobby

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedics assess a man police have found acting erratically on a busy dual carriageway and believe has taken the powerful psychoactive Monkey Dust

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: Contestants must choose one of three ingredients that have inspired the judges during their childhood, including Andy's Vegemite, Mel's cumquats or Jock's panettone

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Dudley, a man collapses while playing bingo two days before his wedding, while in Stoke, crews are called to help an elderly man who has had an unexplained fall.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team are in Corby, Northamptonshire, to complete a build for Jackie and Colin Hutchison and their three children. The eldest, Jordan, has cerebral palsy and epilepsy - but this has not stopped him raising huge sums of money for charity. However, he and his family are currently living with his grandmother after a after a series of building issues left them without the money to complete their own home renovation. As ever, Nick Knowles and the team, plus hundreds of local workers, arrive to change this family's life in just a few short days

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Littlehampton in West Sussex, where a man spent his life savings on home improvements so his sons could visit him but became depressed when rogue builders left the house in a wreck. Local trades, family and friends join the renovators to spend nine days transforming the damp property into a home fit for the future

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team recruit help from the local community to renovate a property in Nottingham. The householder had spent more than a year constructing an extension on to his family home, but died before he could finish it. Local tradespeople, relatives and friends work to transform the property over seven days and turn his legacy into a cosy space for his children

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A French bulldog is brought in after collapsing and struggling to move, and after a full medical assessment, Noel realises an exploded disc in the dog's neck is pressuring its spine to such an extent it is slowly causing paralysis. The vet also treats an excitable Staffordshire bull terrier suffering from a problem where the bones in its elbows do not fit properly and a cat that has been shot with an airgun

programme.title: The Supervet

programme.desc: Pete meets Chow Chow Nugget who has a longer stay than planned, three-legged Tetley, and some Belgium Malinois puppies training to be service dogs

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Award-winning sketch show, featuring the comedienne as an assortment of quirky and oddball characters, with a guest appearance by Siobhan Redmond

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Ex-EastEnders star Jill Halfpenny and sitcom actor Brian Murphy guest star in the sketch show, featuring Catherine Tate as an assortment of bizarre characters

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike hit a new obstacle to nailing down the next event booking when the Captain's past threatens to be unearthed, and he wildly protests at the couple's endeavours. Meanwhile, Thomas and Julian battle it out over how the ghosts will spend their evening, Lady Button is confused by urges she hasn't felt before, and an old friend of Kitty's reappears

programme.title: Aaron Hernandez Uncovered
programme.desc: With so much at stake, why would Aaron Hernandez risk it all? We take a closer look at his life through previously unseen interviews and vivid eyewitness accounts

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Louis spends time with the global pop star Rita Ora. Louis meets Rita at her dad's pub in West London where they sit down for a revealing conversation that takes in her rise to stardom, her family's arrival in the UK in 1990, and the difficulties she has faced dealing with intense press scrutiny and speculation about her personal life. Later, Louis spends time with Rita on the set of her latest music video in LA, before joining her as she puts on a spectacular live performance in Albania

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to a 67-year-old with a lung condition who can't breathe. Plus, crews help a 53-year-old who's had several seizures

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Grace is summoned to a Soho brothel, where a woman is holding a man at knifepoint, claiming he knows the whereabouts of her 16-year-old sister, who has been missing for six months. Revisiting the familiar case, the detective feels guilty she did not do more to help the family earlier. When a body is found, she follows a lead to the missing girl's old teacher, who had acted as her support buddy when she turned to prostitution to pay for her heroin habit, and nearly loses control when she is overcome by anger in the interrogation room. Ian Kelsey guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A convicted murderer reaching the end of his sentence learns his 15-year-old daughter has disappeared and the Sun Hill detectives suspect revenge is involved when investigations reveal she had been going out with the victim's son. Pippa Haywood guest stars as the bitter widow who refuses to co-operate with the inquiry, fearing she may lose her son as well as her husband

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mickey goes undercover as a homeless man to investigate the mystery of a body found in the river wearing a down-and-out's coat over designer clothes. Having eliminated the dead man's ex-fiancee from the inquiries, he gains the trust of a girl living on the streets and discovers the entire homeless community of Sun Hill lives in fear of a shady character known only as Greg. Back at the station, Millie signs off from her final shift

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Conclusion. After another night undercover on the streets, Mickey learns a second down-and-out has been murdered. The fears of the homeless community are underlined when Mickey's informant is badly beaten and suspicion falls on property developer Greg Simm (guest star Ralph Ineson), who has form for fraud and deception. It emerges the suspect had trouble with squatters holding up work on a development site, but evidence against him is hard to find. A bigger shock for Mickey is the news his new friend Nicola is in the frame for the murder

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: On Will Fletcher's last day at Sun Hill, Mickey questions a man accused of assaulting a doctor who he claims is having an affair with his wife. The suspect turns out to be severely depressed and unbalanced, but Callum's macho handling pushes him further toward disaster when he disappears with his baby son in his car. Realising his error, the sergeant tracks him down, only to see the distraught man deliberately smash into a parked vehicle, setting his own car alight

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A 16-year-old claims she has been gang raped by her boyfriend and three of his friends, and Jo goes out on a limb to fight her case, even when it becomes clear the victim is not the innocent she at first appeared. When the girl later asks to drop the charges after receiving threatening telephone calls, Jo begs Terry for more time to extract a confession, determined not to let the arrogant youths get away with their crime

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: An intruder detained in a couple's flat turns out to have been there before, though the residents claim to have no idea who he is. Jo discovers the suspect works as a security guard in the same building as the woman, and investigates the theory he has been stalking her, but extracting a confession puts her in a tricky position. At the end of an arduous shift, she discovers her hard work is to be rewarded with promotion to sergeant. Guest starring Kara Tointon, Barry Sloane and Gareth David-Lloyd

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Return of the comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby. Alison and Mike are trying to find a semi-harmonious routine with the ghostly housemates of Button House. The ghosts have found a way to make their new situation work for them, while the couple are working toward a new money-making venture to fund their perpetual renovations. But after a paranormal photo of the house goes viral, Alison and Mike spot an opportunity to bring money in more quickly by using the ghosts' 'talents' to create an authentic haunted-house experience

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: After a heavy night partying, Alison and Mike wake to find their house in disarray and with the clock ticking until a potentially crucial business partner comes to view the venue, the couple attempt a mass clean up. The ghosts are all acting very strangely, and Alison struggles to piece together the events of the night before to figure out what happened to them

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedics assess a man police have found acting erratically on a busy dual carriageway and believe has taken the powerful psychoactive Monkey Dust

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: Contestants must choose one of three ingredients that have inspired the judges during their childhood, including Andy's Vegemite, Mel's cumquats or Jock's panettone

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Dudley, a man collapses while playing bingo two days before his wedding, while in Stoke, crews are called to help an elderly man who has had an unexplained fall.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team are in Corby, Northamptonshire, to complete a build for Jackie and Colin Hutchison and their three children. The eldest, Jordan, has cerebral palsy and epilepsy - but this has not stopped him raising huge sums of money for charity. However, he and his family are currently living with his grandmother after a after a series of building issues left them without the money to complete their own home renovation. As ever, Nick Knowles and the team, plus hundreds of local workers, arrive to change this family's life in just a few short days

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Littlehampton in West Sussex, where a man spent his life savings on home improvements so his sons could visit him but became depressed when rogue builders left the house in a wreck. Local trades, family and friends join the renovators to spend nine days transforming the damp property into a home fit for the future

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team recruit help from the local community to renovate a property in Nottingham. The householder had spent more than a year constructing an extension on to his family home, but died before he could finish it. Local tradespeople, relatives and friends work to transform the property over seven days and turn his legacy into a cosy space for his children

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A French bulldog is brought in after collapsing and struggling to move, and after a full medical assessment, Noel realises an exploded disc in the dog's neck is pressuring its spine to such an extent it is slowly causing paralysis. The vet also treats an excitable Staffordshire bull terrier suffering from a problem where the bones in its elbows do not fit properly and a cat that has been shot with an airgun

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats 15-stone English mastiff Bear, who has a ruptured ligament in his knee - but as the dog goes into theatre, a major team effort is needed to get this massive patient through surgery. Siamese cat Bluebell is rushed in after being hit by a car and golden retriever Henry is suffering from a chronically damaged spinal disc

programme.title: Classic Car Kings
programme.desc: Irv and the team update a 1977 Daimler Sovereign Coupe giving it a slick, modern makeover. However, a disaster in the paint booth puts the project in peril

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to a private railway, where Simon converts a lantern to a stylish table and Henry turns a fire extinguisher into a steampunk lamp

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: On the evening of December 3, 1926, Agatha Christie left her home in Sunningdale, Berkshire, in her Morris Cowley. Ten days later, Agatha was discovered in a hotel in Harrogate, claiming to have lost her memory. Lucy Worsley delves into this traumatic, watershed moment in Christie's life. She visits the key locations, from the chalk pit where Agatha crashed her car to Abney Hall, the grand house where she took refuge (and which would inspire several of her books)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Mega Metro
programme.desc: Action Stations: We meet the international team taking up the $50 billion trailblazing challenge of building the world's most advanced driverless metro in Sydney

programme.title: Mega Metro
programme.desc: The team faces a tough challenge - boring beneath Sydney's 230-year-old CBD. At Pitt Street Station, eight buildings are demolished, including a 14-storey tower

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: An Aston Martin DB6 in need of total restoration looks set to break the Mathewsons auction record and news of a rare Vincent motorbike arriving causes a stir

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up some 'emerging' sporting classics from the Far East when three Datsun Zs appear, and an unusual lot arrives that run on caterpillar tracks not wheels

programme.title: Could Hitler Have Been Stopped? The Politics of Appeasement
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary covering the debates, discussions and arguments about how and whether Hitler could have been stopped, and the nature of the policy of appeasement prior to and in the early stages of the Second World War. It focuses on the failure of politicians and the governed alike to understand the real threat that the Fuhrer posed

programme.title: Could Hitler Have Been Stopped? The Politics of Appeasement
programme.desc: Historians argue about whether Adolf Hitler could have been stopped before the Second World War ever broke out

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the experts visit Aberglasney Gardens in Carmarthenshire, where they brave the wet weather to examine items including a set of illustrations that was abandoned in a loft, a remnant from the Charge of the Light Brigade and one of the most valuable plates ever seen on the programme

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce takes the team to Bath's Assembly Rooms, where thousands bring their collectibles to be scrutinised. Among the items examined by the experts are a pair of antique bronzes found under a caravan, a marble statue that requires six men to lift, and a genuine work by Constable

programme.title: Bangers & Cash

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries

programme.title: Equator

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: On the evening of December 3, 1926, Agatha Christie left her home in Sunningdale, Berkshire, in her Morris Cowley. Ten days later, Agatha was discovered in a hotel in Harrogate, claiming to have lost her memory. Lucy Worsley delves into this traumatic, watershed moment in Christie's life. She visits the key locations, from the chalk pit where Agatha crashed her car to Abney Hall, the grand house where she took refuge (and which would inspire several of her books)

programme.title: Could Hitler Have Been Stopped? The Politics of Appeasement
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary covering the debates, discussions and arguments about how and whether Hitler could have been stopped, and the nature of the policy of appeasement prior to and in the early stages of the Second World War. It focuses on the failure of politicians and the governed alike to understand the real threat that the Fuhrer posed

programme.title: Could Hitler Have Been Stopped? The Politics of Appeasement

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Early morning chat show presented by Lorraine Kelly, with celebrity interviews, entertainment news, cooking tips, fashion features and musical guests.

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The concluding part of an interview with the former Republic of Ireland star, as he discusses with Tommy Martin the high esteem he was held in for club and country while facing battles on and off the pitch

programme.title: Jann
programme.desc: Jann, Nate, Max, and Dave go on a romantic mountain getaway, but Max has a problem with Jann's new boyfriend. Canadian comedy series, starring singer-songwriter Jann Arden

programme.title: Jann
programme.desc: An eccentric wealthy couple offers Jann the money to fund her new album in exchange for promoting their gender reveal company. Canadian comedy series, starring Jann Arden

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade heads to Russia's remote Amur River, where the mysterious deaths of three men appear to be linked to a freshwater giant - a beast that can exceed 18ft in length and weigh more than a ton. However, he encounters a wall of silence and begins to suspect he has stumbled on to a dark secret that isn't supposed to be uncovered

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Culinary expert Frances Atkins tests three home cooks who are tasked with making a German pasta called spaetzle. Sheree Murphy hosts

programme.title: The Six O'Clock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants John, Maria, Caroline and Mark vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and designer Danny Clarke have just one day to transform a grey and windswept roof terrace in Basingstoke, Hampshire into a cosy and colourful outdoor room for owner Aimee

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: The show visits Walthamstow in east London, where interior designer Linda Barker provides loving mum and caring sister Vikki Ward with a home makeover. Presented by Peter Andre

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder tells the story of Frances Molloy, whose 18-year-old son was killed in a coach crash in 2012, leading her to fight for changes to the law, making it illegal for buses and coaches to use tyres that are more than 10 years old. He also examines the 1973 case of David McGreavy, who murdered three children he was supposed to be looking after

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Experts analyse the work of engineers including Sir Nigel Gresley, who engineered the world's fastest steam train in 1938 and Eric Laithwaite, whose linear motor made frictionless travel a possibility. These innovators influenced the engineering of the Shanghai Maglev, the fastest passenger train in operation on Earth, which broke numerous engineering boundaries in its construction. Held in place by a series of electromagnets, it levitates on an air gap of 10mm and is able to reach a top speed of 431kms per hour

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Terry from Bedlington, Verity from Bedford, Liam from Cardiff and Barbara from London pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Roy from Fareham, Danny from Sheffield, Eileen from London and Carmen from Halifax pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: My Mum, Your Dad
programme.desc: Parents channel the wild west for a hoedown party with some intimate conversations leading to a first kiss. One of the mums has an emotional breakthrough during a game, and another plucks up the courage to ask a dad on a date, with a surprising outcome. Davina McCall hosts

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean
programme.desc: Captain Sandy embarks on a new charter season on the beautiful waters of the Italian Riviera, but two of her stews have extreme issues getting to the yacht

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Children are filmed in a school specially rigged with hidden cameras. In the studio, host Jason Manford challenges parents to predict what their child will do in each situation, providing an insight into the developing minds of five to seven year-olds. The family with the most correct predictions goes through to the final. And the kids choose the prizes

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi heads to Crawley in West Sussex to help out a family-run concern just five miles away from Gatwick. Despite its proximity to the busy airport, the Waterhall Country House Hotel is struggling and owners Anne and Andy Marsh desperately need a change in fortunes if it is to become profitable. Can Alex set them on the way to success?

programme.title: Alan Quinlan: Red Blooded
programme.desc: Part two of two. Alan Quinlan talks to Joe Molloy about his Ireland career. The back-row forward won 27 international caps during a nine-year period full of ups and downs. In the second part, he discusses becoming only the second player to reach 200 appearances for Munster and how he dealt with anxiety and mental health throughout his rugby career

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager sits down with Tommy Martin. In the first part, he discusses his playing days, including the glory years of Ireland's World Cup campaigns

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The next generation of engineering wonders, beginning with an international team in Norway who are building the largest polar expedition vessel in the world

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A patient who set fire to his own shop (guest star Tony Slattery) attempts to avoid police questioning by leaping into an ambulance and driving off towards the pier - unaware Alun and a sick man are in the back of the vehicle. Meanwhile, Dr Makori fears for her relationship with Dr Fox when he tells her he has been offered a job in Biafra. Medical drama, with Andy Wear, Paul Fox and Kananu Kirimi

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A wedding reception held on Elsinby Pier ends in tragedy when a fishing trawler sails badly off course. Meanwhile, Adam faces up to a tough decision and the staff at the hospital prepare for a new arrival as Jill goes into labour. Robert Cavanah and Amy Robbins star

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson helps sisters Sharon and Joy with their struggling country house hotel in Worlington, Suffolk. While the Grade-II listed building is in good repair, the operating systems are chaotic and the menu too extensive for the solitary chef to handle. Ruth organises a revamp of the dated-looking function room - prompting the delighted owners to plan a wedding fair in their new-look space

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines real-life cases, looking at the events leading up to crimes, going through the police investigations and summing up the trials

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: The pressure is on when strange things start happening to Cassie, Abigail and Joy's abilities. Fantasy, starring Catherine Bell

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie, Abigail, Joy and George come together to protect the Merriwick magic from the mysterious force over Middleton. Cassie and Sam make a momentous decision about their life together

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade visits southern Africa to see if he can track down a deadly fish that lurks in the eerie waters of the Okavango. A killer with shark-like teeth, there are stories of it hunting in packs and feeding on schooling fish or even human bodies. Jeremy is determined to find out if it is a giant piranha or something equally terrifying

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: A lurcher arrives at Battersea Dogs and Cats Home with a terrible injury in need of urgent surgery. Plus, Motherland stars Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan help Paul with a cavalier King Charles spaniel, and calm down an over excited Frenchie pup

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets some more weird and wonderful hounds at Battersea, including a large bull mastiff with some very particular demands. He is also introduced to a pair of chihuahua pug crosses with a strange family dynamic and an unusual looking mongrel who is very shy. Can Paul bring the timid pooch out of its shell?

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Jason Manford presents the show which asks how well people really know their kids, by challenging parents to guess what their children will do in a variety of entertaining situations. Each show sees youngsters filmed while taking part in a variety of classroom activities and games, unaware that we're actually testing everything from empathy and lateral thinking to curiosity and rule breaking

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen
programme.desc: The return of the American version of Gordon Ramsay's culinary boot camp. The 22nd series begins with contestants competing in the signature dish challenge after being divided into two teams and the winning team being rewarded with a private dinner party with chef Ramsay while the losing team are left behind to clean up.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen
programme.desc: Tensions run high when the contestants must deliberate on which chef makes the best scallops and a rocky first dinner service for both teams involves raw meat and bad communication - however, Chef Ramsay must decide who will not make the cut

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: Daisy is overwhelmed with the high-maintenance guests and struggles to ask for help, so a request from Gary sends her over the edge

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing: LA
programme.desc: Flagg creates a marketing video with a special guest to promote a house in Pasadena, and Tracy encounters an surprise at a penthouse listing.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean
programme.desc: Hannah scrambles to manage the situation as bad weather rolls in, while Captain Sandy and Malia draw a hard line with the deck team

programme.title: Make Ireland Great Again
programme.desc: Richard Chambers looks at the rapidly changing relationship betweek Ireland and the US and the tensions driving a rift down the Atlantic between the countries

programme.title: A Mother's Son
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part drama, starring Hermione Norris, Martin Clunes, Paul McGann and Alexander Arnold. In the aftermath of the murder and the discovery of the bloodstained trainers, Rosie's distress starts to affect her family life and cracks begin to form. Meanwhile, David has learnt their son Jamie has been mixing with violent dropout Sean and fears he may have fallen under his influence
